title: 'The Big Homecoming' and Wells Fargo Kick-off Year Two of Relationship To Create Ongoing Impact For HBCUs
text: ATLANTA , Aug. 31, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- The nation's newest premier music and culture festival,  The Big Homecoming (TBH) alongside founding corporate supporter Wells Fargo, will kick off year two of its programming with The Big Homecoming Music & Culture Festival and The Big Homecoming 365 Impact Tour (TBH365). The Big Homecoming, which launched in Atlanta in 2022, had enormous success in its first year, with the mission of authentically celebrating the culture of Historically Black Colleges and Universities (HBCUs) while creating ongoing impact for HBCUs.

title: Bank ETFs Had a Downbeat August: What Lies Ahead?
text: Banking stocks had a downbeat August. While situation is still grave for regional bank ETFs, still-cheaper valuation and decent earnings for big banks are the positives.

title: Here's the danger for investors in holding too much cash, says Wells Fargo
text: For the first time in ages, it pays — even after inflation — to hold cash. But high yields could disguise a pitfall for investors, warned an investment strategist at the Wells Fargo Investment Institute.

title: Wells Fargo (WFC) to Pay $35M Penalty to SEC & Settle Charges
text: Wells Fargo (WFC) to pay a penalty of $35 million for overcharging advisory fees in more than 10,900 investment advisory accounts. It paid around $40 million to the distressed accountholders.

title: Wells Fargo pays $35M fine for charging excessive advisory fees: SEC
text: Wells Fargo has reportedly been overcharging nearly 11,000 investment accounts over the past decade. Without admitting to the violation, the bank agreed to pay the SEC $35 million.

title: Wells Fargo Looks Set For Further Outperformance
text: Stronger capital and deposit positioning has continued to help Wells Fargo, driving better-than-expected Q2'23 earnings and outperformance relative to most large bank peers. Wells Fargo still has work to do to resolve outstanding consent orders, but there are operational leverage opportunities on both the revenue and cost sides of the business. Wells Fargo has opportunities for operational improvement, including reducing costs, enhancing digital offerings, and expanding its card and wealth management businesses.

title: Wells Fargo Agrees to Pay $35M to SEC Over Excessive Customer Charges
text: Wells Fargo (WFC) has agreed to pay $35 million to the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) over charges of excessive advisory fees, the SEC said.

title: Wells Fargo to pay $35 million in a civil penalty
text: Wells Fargo & Co (NYSE: WFC) has returned a total of $40 million to 10,945 of its customers who were overcharged for investment advice, as per the Securities & Exchange Commission.

title: Wells Fargo agrees to pay $35M to settle charges it didn't provide promised broker fee discounts to clients
text: The Securities and Exchange Commission on Friday said Wells Fargo & Co. WFC, -1.05% agreed to pay a $35 million civil penalty to settle claims that its Wells Fargo Clearing Services LLC and Wells Fargo Advisors Financial Network LLC units overcharged thousands of its investment advisory accounts. The SEC said the bank levied excessive fees of a combined $26.8 million on more than 10,900 investment advisory accounts, prior to 2014.

title: Wells Fargo: Dividend Increase And Buyback Do Go Far
text: Wells Fargo has increased its dividend by nearly 17% and announced a $30 billion buyback program following a successful stress test. Wells Fargo's dividend coverage and related metrics mostly compare favorably against its peers. Wells Fargo's leadership in the middle-market is attractive.

title: Wells Fargo to pay $35mln penalty over excessive fees, U.S. SEC says
text: Wells Fargo has agreed to pay a $35 million civil penalty to settle U.S. charges that the company overcharged advisory fees, the Securities and Exchange Commission said on Friday.

title: Wells Fargo says customers are experiencing 'issues with certain transactions' amid possible outage
text: Wells Fargo said that customers are experiencing issues with some transactions Thursday, according to a company spokesperson, amid reports of people being unable to access certain account features.

title: Hundreds of Wells Fargo users report issues with banking system
text: More than 700 users reported issues with Wells Fargo's banking system Thursday, according to Downdetector, with problems ranging from transferring funds to declining ATM cards.

title: Judge dismisses lawsuit against Wells Fargo for allegedly holding fake job interviews for diversity candidates
text: U.S. district judge Trina L. Thompson has dismissed a lawsuit against Wells Fargo & Co. WFC, +0.05% that alleged the bank held fake job interviews for positions that were already filled in order to boost its diverse hiring score card.

title: Wells Fargo & Company (WFC) is Attracting Investor Attention: Here is What You Should Know
text: Wells Fargo (WFC) has received quite a bit of attention from Zacks.com users lately. Therefore, it is wise to be aware of the facts that can impact the stock's prospects.

title: CURO Subsidiary Sued for Misleading Consumer Loan Borrowers
text: CURO's subsidiary, Heights Finance, faces lawsuit for deceptively pushing struggling borrowers to refinance short-term loans and placing them in continuous debt.

title: Stock Market Crash Alert: S&P Bank Downgrades Rock Stocks
text: A number of major American banks are in the red today following S&P Globals' credit ratings adjustments on several regional lenders yesterday. Fears of a stock market crash are swirling as equity markets continue to show some sheepishness heading further into the second half of the year.

title: Wells Fargo (WFC) Wins Class Action Suit Over Fake Interviews
text: Wells Fargo (WFC) wins the dismissal of the lawsuit accusing it of faking interviews to meet its guidelines on workplace diversity.

title: Wells Fargo (WFC) Aided by Cost Control Amid Lower Revenues
text: Cost-saving initiatives and strong deposit growth are likely to keep supporting Wells Fargo's (WFC) financials. However, a decline in revenues and a fall in loan balance are major headwinds.

title: Wells Fargo defeats shareholder lawsuit over fake job interviews
text: Wells Fargo won the dismissal of a lawsuit accusing the fourth-largest U.S. bank of defrauding shareholders by touting its commitment to hiring diversity while its managers were conducting sham interviews of non-white and female job candidates.

title: Brokers Suggest Investing in Wells Fargo (WFC): Read This Before Placing a Bet

title: Goldman Sachs still a buy, bank analyst says, despite noise around CEO David Solomon
text: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. Chief Executive David Solomon should stay in the job despite recent criticism and even as the bank's stock performance has lagged its peers, Odeon Capital analyst Dick Bove said on Friday.

title: Wells Fargo & Company Declares Cash Dividends on Preferred Stock
text: SAN FRANCISCO--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Wells Fargo & Company (NYSE: WFC) today announced dividends on eight series of preferred stock. A quarterly cash dividend of $18.75 per share was declared on its 7.50% noncumulative perpetual convertible class A preferred stock, Series L, liquidation preference $1,000 per share, which is traded on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol “WFCPrL”. The Series L dividend is payable on Sept. 15, 2023, to holders of record as of the close of business on Aug.

title: Are Major U.S. Banks At Risk Of Credit-Ratings Downgrades?
text: Shares of the biggest banks in the U.S., including JPMorgan Chase & Co. NYSE: JPM, Bank of America NYSE: BAC, Citigroup Inc. NYSE: C, Wells Fargo & Co. NYSE: WFC and Morgan Stanley NYSE: MS traded lower on August 16, continuing sector declines that began earlier in the week.

title: Are You Missing Out on This Bank's Monster Dividend Raise?
text: One of the U.S.'s Big Four lenders hiked its quarterly payout by almost 17%. That's a much higher bump than the declared and intended raises of the other three.

title: UBS to Pay $1.44B to Clear 2008 Financial Crisis Case with DOJ
text: UBS agrees to pay $1.44 billion to settle financial crisis case with the U.S. DOJ. UBS asserted that the settlement has been fully provisioned in prior periods.

title: UBS, Credit Suisse Banned From Russia Subsidiary Disposals
text: A Moscow court bans UBS Group (UBS) and Credit Suisse from disposing of shares in their Russia subsidiaries after a request by Russia's Zenit Bank.

title: Fitch warns multiple US banks face credit downgrade: report
text: A Fitch Ratings analyst warned the agency may be forced to downgrade dozens of U.S. banks, including major lenders like JPMorgan Chase and Bank of America.

title: UBS to Face Another Lawsuit for Inadequate Consideration
text: UBS is expected to face another claim by former shareholders and employees of Credit Suisse who suffered losses due to the emergency takeover of the latter.

title: Stock Market Crash Alert: Michael Burry Is Shorting the Market
text: Fears of a stock market crash are swirling following the release of a 13F filing that shows Scion Asset Management, brainchild of Big Short star Michael Burry, shorted both the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite during Q2 of this year, along with selling nearly its entire holding of bank stocks. Indeed, the hedge fund has apparently taken a notably pessimistic view of U.S. financial markets as of recently.

title: Fitch warns it may have to downgrade slew of US banks including giants like JPMorgan
text: Fitch Ratings in June cut its "operating environment" score for the US banking sector. Fitch analyst Chris Wolfe told CNBC that another downgrade would mean banking giants like JPMorgan face reevaluations.

title: U.S. bank stocks drop after Fitch downgrade warning
text: Shares of U.S. banks dropped in premarket trading after a Fitch Ratings analyst told CNBC that the agency could downgrade several lenders, while a proposed rule change by a top banking regulator added to investor worries.

title: Wells Fargo & Company Announces Full Redemption of its Series Q Preferred Stock and Related Depositary Shares
text: SAN FRANCISCO--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Wells Fargo & Company (NYSE: WFC) today announced that on Sept. 15, 2023, it will redeem all 69,000 shares (the “Redeemed Series Q Preferred Shares”) of its 5.85% Fixed-to-Floating Rate Non-Cumulative Perpetual Class A Preferred Stock, Series Q (the “Series Q Preferred Stock”). The redemption of the Redeemed Series Q Preferred Shares will trigger the redemption of the 69,000,000 shares of the related depositary shares (the “Redeemed Series Q Depositary Shares.

title: Big US banks are reportedly trying to dump commercial real estate loans - but buyers are scarce as pressures mount for property markets
text: Big banks are trying to dump commercial real estate loans as pressures mount in the sector.  JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs, and Capital One are among those trying to shed debt exposure, sources told Bloomberg.

title: Moody's Slashes Ratings on Top US Banks: 5 ‘Strong Buy' Dividend Giants Are the Only Safe Plays Now
text: They assured us after the failure of Silicon Valley Bank, First Republic and Signature Bank that all was well, that the banking system was totally secure and in no danger of further failures.

title: US regulators fine Wall Street firms $549 million in latest texting probe
text: US regulators on Tuesday fined nine Wall Street companies, including Wells Fargo, BNP Paribas and Société Générale $549 million over employees' use of personal messaging apps to discuss deals, trades and other business.

title: Wells Fargo, BNP among firms fined $260 million over use of WhatsApp, texts
text: Regulators issued fines against several financial firms, including Wells Fargo and BNP Paribas, for failing to maintain records of employee communications on unapproved platforms.

title: Wells Fargo's $125 Million Fine For Employees Using WhatsApp Marks Latest Run-In With Regulators And Courts
text: Wells Fargo's history is riddled with multi-million dollar legal settlements and billions of dollars paid to regulators.

title: Moody's Downgrades Credit Ratings For 10 Banks, Puts Six Others On Notice
text: Ratings agency Moody's cut the credit ratings of several small and midsized banks late Monday and warned other big lenders, citing profitability challenges and funding risks.

title: US banks head south rattled by Moody's downgrades
text: US banking shares prices tumbled on Tuesday as credit ratings agency Moody's cut the credit ratings of a number of small and mid-sized lenders and placed several big Wall Street names on negative review.  The credit ratings agency downgraded the ratings of 10 banks by one rung, while major lenders Bank of New York Mellon, US Bancorp, State Street, Truist Financial, Cullen/Frost Bankers and Northern Trust are now under review for a potential downgrade.

title: Why Are Bank Stocks Down Today?
text: Bank stocks are falling on Tuesday and investors can look to Moody's lowering credit ratings as the reason for the decline. The credit rating business started off the bad news for bank stocks by cutting credit ratings for 10 banks.

title: Wells Fargo to pay largest fine in SEC recordkeeping enforcement action
text: Wells Fargo & Co. WFC, -2.72% will pay $125 million out of a total of $289 million in fines by the Securities and Exchange Commission against 10 firms “for widespread and longstanding failures by the firms and their employees to maintain and preserve electronic communications,” the SEC said. The firms have started to carry out improvements to their compliance policies and procedures to address the violations, the SEC said.

title: Citigroup, Wells Fargo, PNC: Undervalued Dividend Growers
text: If you're exploring dividend stocks, you can adopt various strategies. You might seek out high-yield stocks, or those with consistent payouts and robust financial conditions, or companies with a track record of boosting dividends.

title: Moody's warns it could cut credit ratings of 6 big US banks
text: Moody's has put the credit ratings of six large US banks, including Bank of New York Mellon, State Street and Northern Trust, under review for a possible downgrade.

title: Wells Fargo hires Credit Suisse veteran as head of financial sponsors
text: U.S. bank Wells Fargo said on Monday Malcolm Price has joined its corporate and investment bank (CIB) as the head of financial sponsors, after 35 years at Credit Suisse.

title: Wells Fargo says missing deposits glitch is resolved
text: The Wells Fargo glitch that made some customers' direct deposits disappear from their bank accounts is over, the bank said on Saturday.

title: Wells Fargo fixing tech issue after customers report missing deposit transactions
text: Wells Fargo said Friday that it has been working to resolve a technical issue that caused some customers' deposit transactions to disappear from their accounts.

title: Europe's Largest Bank Raises Full-Year Guidance, Boosts Dividend
text: Europe's largest bank fell along with the broader global market on August 2, despite reporting strong year-over-year revenue and earnings growth the day before.

title: Customers report missing deposits from Wells Fargo bank accounts
text: Wells Fargo is dealing with a technical issue that has resulted in customers reporting that their direct deposits had disappeared from their bank accounts.

title: BofA holds No. 1 position in corporate bond underwriting, with Wells Fargo moving up to No.
text: Bank of America Corp.'s investment bank BofA Securities remained the top corporate debt issuer in 2023 as of Aug. 1, while Wells Fargo Securities LLC has bumped JPMorgan Chase & Co. for the No. 2 slot, according to S&P Global Market Intelligence data.

title: JPMorgan, Goldman and Morgan Stanley win praise in Jefferies Q2 bank earnings wrap
text: Bank fundamentals were challenged but results were better than worst-case fears as higher interest rates boosted bank loan yields, Jefferies analyst Ken Usdin said Thursday in the firm's summary of second-quarter profits for the 36 large and mid-sized banks in its coverage universe.

title: Why Investors Should Be Loving C.F. Industries This Summer
text: As the United States economy continues to show signs of a potential contraction, especially after following the nine-month consecutive contracting readings in the ISM manufacturing PMI index, some industries are beginning to show signs of favoritism. As the cycle gets underway, markets usually switch their preferences between the industries deemed 'cyclical' in and / or out of those opposed as 'defensives.

title: Wells Fargo pledges $60 million for worker re-entry program
text: Wells Fargo on Wednesday said it may commit $60 million to Concordance, a nonprofit organization that helps workers with criminal records re-enter the workforce.

title: Wells Fargo Aims to Provide up to $60 Million to Concordance Anti-Recidivism Program
text: Concordance says re-entry program cuts reincarceration by 56%, builds financial health for participants COLUMBIA, S.C. , Aug. 2, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.

title: Wells Fargo, Bank of America to pay FDIC up to $3.7 billion combined for bank failure special assessment
text: Wells Fargo & Co. WFC, -1.54% said it will pay up to $1.8 billion to the Federal Deposit Insurance Co.'s deposit insurance fund as part of the government's special assessment following the regional-bank crisis earlier this year. Wells Fargo said it will expense the entire amount upon the FDIC's finalization of the rule.

title: Wells Fargo warns capital rules will prompt it to rejig balance sheet
text: Wells Fargo said on Tuesday that proposed U.S. capital rules could lead the bank to rejig its balance sheet.
